observations.recommendation:
To enhance asset management and address the noted deficiencies, the following
measures are recommended, considering the agency’s operating realities:

a. Conduct One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts.
b. Formulate and implement an Asset Management Framework by developing
and institutionalizing an Asset Management Manual.
c. Sanctions for Non-Compliance.
d. Reassess manpower complement for the GSO Designate and match it with the
existing operational and regulatory demands and workloads. Also, implement a
capability- building program that includes regular trainings relevant to asset
management and financial reporting.
e. Ensure the conduct and completion of the annual physical count of PPE and
submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team.
f. Reconstitute a Disposal Committee to perform the disposal activities for
unserviceable PPEs.
g. Enhance compliance with the Property Insurance Law by insuring its
permanent buildings with GSIS.

To address the identified gaps, we recommended and Management agreed to the
following courses of action:

a. Thorough Review and Identification: Conduct a detailed review of each project
with outstanding balances to determine their current status and level of
completion.
b. Utilization of Funds for Ongoing Projects: For projects that are still in progress,
prioritize the use of the available funds to ensure their timely completion.
c. Proper Disposition of Funds for Completed Projects: For projects that have been
completed but still have unspent balances, seek authorization from the respective
source agencies on how to utilize these savings. If authorization is not granted,
the unspent amounts should be promptly returned to the source agencies as
required by regulations.
d. Effective Implementation of Future Projects: For all upcoming projects funded
by NGAs, ensure diligent planning, execution, and monitoring to maximize the
utilization of financial resources and achieve the intended outcomes.
e. Corrective Accounting Adjustments: Make the necessary adjustments in the
accounting records to reclassify the items that were incorrectly recorded under
the "Due to NGAs" account to their appropriate liability or revenue accounts.
This will ensure that the financial statements accurately reflect the Municipality's
financial position.

1. We recommended that the management further improve the level of its compliance with the rules of RA 9184 and the quality of its procurement processes to ensure transparency and competitiveness in government procurement through adoption of appropriate measures, as far as practicable, that would reinforce the faithful observance of the required activities in the conduct of competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and to promptly submit perfected contracts and other required supporting documents to facilitate timely review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Invitations for observers are now made in writing during the post-qualification process; Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed are now posted on PhilGEPS to further promote transparency in all stages of the procurement; Notification to the losing bidders of the BAC's recommendation are also now made in writing; The Bids and Awards Committee are doing its best that all copies of contracts entered into by the municipality are submitted to COA based on the prescribed timeline.

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended that the management observe proper compliance with the recording processes, taking into consideration the applicable standards and laws, to reflect the correct balances and timing of recognition of each account.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments have been made through journal entries to properly record and report the fund transfers from the National Government Agencies.

observations.recommendation:
3. Taking up the necessary accounting/adjusting entries to recognize the change in accounting policy.
4. Strengthening controls over PPE and semi-expendable property by maintaining the necessary forms, registry, and reports as prescribed in COA Circular 2022-004, dated May 31, 2022, and revisit the related issuances to further improve recording and reporting processes.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments have been made through journal entries to reclassify the semi-expendable Property, Plant and Equipment; Necessary forms, registries and other documentary requirements are now being maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant as well as the Property and Supply Officer.

observations.recommendation:
5. Utilize LDRRM Funds in accordance with the purpose and framework outlined in Republic Act (R.A.) No. 10121, Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1, and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
6. Revisit and improve the preparation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan by including comprehensive programs, projects, and activities that address DRRM objectives, such as capability building and structures to reduce or mitigate disaster risks in vulnerable communities or areas within the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Comprehensive planning of the different PPAs that would address DRRM objectives is being considered; Currently the municipality is on the process of endorsing to the MDRRMC the insurance of the municipality's buildings and other structures; The municipality earnestly follow the guidelines set by the DILG and OCD to make sure that no constituent will be left behind in accessing assistance from the Office of the MDRRM through quick response and well-deserved and profound services before, during and after emergencies.

observations.recommendation:
7. Require the AO to liquidate previous cash advances for CIF amounting to P3,210,750.00 before granting new cash advance.
8. Issue demand letter to AOs for the liquidation of P38,930,841.92, granted to officers and for operating expenses.
9. Refrain/stop the granting of CA without legally specific purpose.
10. Stop the practice of granting CA to elected officials other than for official traveling expense, pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Strict observance and imposition by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer Accountant of the existing guidelines and policies on cash advances are now being practiced which resulted to a hugedecrease in the amount of unliquidated cash advances. Internal control has been established to provide safeguard on resources of the municipality. Follow-ups had been made thru demand letters to the different accountable officers and employees to liquidate their cash advances.

Ensure that timely and accurate RPT assessments are made through managing the factors affecting the availability of updated real property valuations by initiating, where practicable through its Sanggunian, the periodic revision of fair market values of real properties within the Municipality; Improve the collection efficiency by participating in the revenue setting process to come up with more realistic targets, employing effective strategies to optimally collect tax receivables, and avail the remedies set forth by the LGC in coordination with the PTO; Enhance its operating practices to further reinforce controls over its RPT and SET collections through institutionalizing measures that would ensure that RPT and SET collections are promptly remitted by collectors, and immediately deposited to an AGDB; and Enhance the reliability of its financial representation by instituting arrangements to ensure that RPT/SET are appropriately recognized in the year it was earned. To reclassify the prior years’ income and to correct the current year RPT and SET income accounts, we also recommended Proposed Adjusting Journal Entries.

observations.recommendation: 15. We recommended and the Management agreed to further improve, where practicable, the quality of its 20% DF initiatives delivery in order to ensure the actualization of its development goals by appropriately managing the factors affecting planning and implementation through incorporating updated data on its PPA proposals, setting of project timelines, backstopping of adequate manpower, addressing issues relating to job order pay arrangement, and enhancing its compliance with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Management agreed to further improve, where practicable, the quality of its 20% DF initiatives delivery in order to ensure the actualization of its development goals by appropriately managing the factors affecting planning and implementation through incorporating updated data on its PPA proposals, setting of project timelines, backstopping of adequate manpower, addressing issues relating to job order pay arrangement, and enhancing its compliance with RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure the quality of results delivery of its PPAs funded by fund transfers by further improving its management processes through compilation of related project documents, use of updated and reliable data, and enhanced project monitoring and evaluation practices.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure the quality of results delivery of its PPAs funded by fund transfers by further improving its management processes through compilation of related project documents, use of updated and reliable data, and enhanced project monitoring and evaluation practices.

The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; prepare/maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card; Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and disclose the total network system in the Notes to Financial Statements; The General Services Office should also maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and render a Report on Local Road Network of the LGU at the end of the accounting period; and Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the accounting office and the general services office with complete description and cost segregation of road components.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to implement the policies and
guidelines relating to recording and reporting PPE, semi-expendable property, and
their related accounts by:

in accounting policy, as follows:

Accounts Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustments / Government
Equity xxx
Semi-expendable Property xxx

years and issued to end-users.

b. Strengthening controls over PPE and semi-expendable property by
maintaining the necessary forms, registry, and reports as prescribed in COA
Circular 2022-004, dated May 31, 2022, and revisit the related issuances to
further improve recording and reporting processes.

Road Networks 7,416,568.36

We recommended that the management further improve the level of its

We recommended for Management to adopt mechanisms to ensure adherence
and compliance to the guidelines on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash
advances, particularly on the following:

officers and for operating expenses.

c. Refrain/stop the granting of CA without legally specific purpose.

d. Stop the practice of granting CA to elected officials other than for official
traveling expense, pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.

With all the matters discussed, we recommended and the Management agreed to:

improving its management processes through compilation of related project documents,

developmental projects; and maintenance of adequate
records that will facilitate reliable and fair

and revisit and follow policy guidelines on

Fully Implemented
We recommended and Management agreed to enhance
the delivery of efficient and effective COVID-19
response, rehabilitation, and recovery initiatives by
improving the quality of related management
processes leading to a more inclusive planning, detailed
budgeting, implementation, and monitoring of results
by incorporating lessons learned about COVID-19
pandemic, defining expenditures in terms of specific
PPAs before funding, and using relevant and reliable
data to guide decision-making.

In view of the foregoing discourse, we recommended and Management agreed to
ensure the effective and efficient utilization and reporting of its loans by further
enhancing the related management processes through inclusive planning that will
sufficiently articulate well-thought out and prioritized developmental projects; and
maintenance of adequate records that will facilitate reliable and fair representations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that between and among LGUs the LGUs of the province further enhance the quality of its individual LDRRM management processes by taking concrete actions to address the issues related to manpower, data and information, and formalization to reinforce the establishment of an enabling level of coordination in a collective setting and ensure disaster resiliency.
observations.managementAction: *Strong coordination in the province is made sure to ensure a sustainable and responsive delivery of services to the constituents. All PPAs are aligned to the PPAs of the Provincial DRRM Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to revisit the guidelines on the provision of funds for all programs, projects, and activities to address the COVID-19 situation especially on budget realignment and revision of MDRRMFIP to ensure that procedural controls are not bypassed.
We also recommended and the management concurred to:
a. review its current practices in relation to the distribution matrix and accounting of inventory held for distribution. And ending inventory should be recognized for the remaining stocks of food and hygiene packs by determining its cost and making necessary adjusting entry;
b. follow procedural controls on the accounting and reporting of cash and in-kind donations; and
c. improve the management of relief goods by establishing a more defined and stronger controls.
observations.managementAction:
*The MDRRM Council has decided to prioritize different PPAs in responding to and recovering from disaster. The management in coordination with the Local Sanggunian, made sure that realignments and reprogramming of different PPAs are supported with SB ordinances and resolutions.
*Proper inventory of goods is now being practiced.
Aside from relief logbooks, stock cards are now being maintained to establish stronger controls and to ensure proper accounting and recording in the inventory of goods.
And agreed that all donations received form external sources will be properly accounted and recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to enhance its current PPE internal control activities through maintenance of up-to-date PPE register/records, establishment and strengthening of appropriate property custodianship practices, and periodic conduct of physical asset verification and reconciliation to reinforce safeguard of resources, physical accountability over assets and preparation of reliable reports.
observations.managementAction: *Continued efforts by the GSO-designate to conduct physical inventory of all properties of the municipality. The disposal team is currently evaluating the current status of PPE to determine whether they are still serviceable or not.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management, through its Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO, agreed to –
a. identify all equipment used for response and rescue activities, and properly determine the corresponding costs and accumulated depreciation;
b. subsequently, cause the reclassification of said equipment to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account; and
c. revisit guidelines of COA Circular 2012-002 on proper accounting and reporting of equipment to which are used for response and rescue operations of the Municipality as well proper treatment of other items mentioned therein.
observations.managementAction: *Identified all PPE used for disaster, response and rescue activities and reclassified them to the proper account. An adjustment was made to some assets capitalized charging them to expense since these were distributed/donated directly to the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures to reinforce the preparation of accurate and reliable reports/financial statements and improve practices which the Management agreed to consider:
a. accrue the interest charges of the existing loan contracts to present the correct interest expenses of the borrowings for the year;
b. appropriately present the current portion of loans payable which are due to be settled within twelve months after the balance sheet date under Current Liabilities category in the Statement of Financial Position; and
c. strengthen commitment through faithful adherence with the requirements set forth in IPSAS 1 to ensure the fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made to recognize accruals. The current portion of loans payable is already presented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure, as far as practicable, an optimum level of delivery of 20% DF initiatives through institution of appropriate arrangements and mechanisms that will effectively manage matters affecting implementation, and enhance aspects relating to periodic monitoring and communication.
observations.managementAction: *Prior year PPAs are now prioritized for implementation which resulted to increase in the percentage of completion. Infrastructure projects are now done thru “Contracting” or “Bidding” to fast track the implementation. The LCE also created a functional Project Monitoring Team to ensure that PPAs are being properly implemented and delivered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management concurred to establish necessary operating modalities that will effectively address concerns affecting the timeliness, accuracy and reliability of 20% DF reporting in order to reinforce accountability.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the status of implementation of 20% DF PPAs promptly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management with the Sanggunian as leads, formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: GFPS and TWG have convened to formulate the GAD Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, with GFPS as leads, ensure, as far as practicable, an optimum level of delivery of GAD initiatives through the establishment of GAD monitoring and evaluation mechanisms that exacts the performance of responsibilities and enhances the periodic monitoring of implementation, in accordance with the requirements of Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: LGU will instituted and enhanced additional GAD mechanism to achieve the delivery of GAD initiatives.

*The Head of Agency will require the Municipal Engineering Office to regularly submit needed documents to the Municipal Accountant and GSO in order to maintain records pertaining to the Local Road Network and render a report every end of fiscal year.
observations.reason: Non-functional GSO Designate

enhance the quality of its individual LDRRM management processes by taking concrete
actions to address the issues related to manpower, data and information, and
formalization to reinforce the establishment of an enabling level of coordination in a
collective setting and ensure disaster resiliency.

We recommended and the Management agreed to revisit the
guidelines on the provision of funds for programs, projects, and activities to address the
COVID-19 situation especially on budget realignment and revision of MDRRMFIP to
ensure that procedural controls are not bypassed.

periodic conduct of physical asset verification and reconciliation to reinforce safeguard
of resources, physical accountability over assets and preparation of reliable reports.

b. subsequently, cause the reclassification of said equipment to Disaster Response

c. revisit guidelines of COA Circular 2012-002 on proper accounting and

We recommended the following measures to reinforce the preparation of accurate

a. accrue the interest charges of the existing loan contracts to present the correct
interest expenses of the borrowings for the year;
b. appropriately present the current portion of loans payable which are due to
be settled within twelve months after the balance sheet date under Current
Liabilities category in the Statement of Financial Position; and

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure, as far as practicable,
an optimum level of delivery of 20% DF initiatives through institution of appropriate
arrangements and mechanisms that will effectively manage matters affecting
implementation, and enhance aspects relating to periodic monitoring and
communication.

We recommended and the Management concurred to establish necessary
operating modalities that will effectively address concerns affecting the timeliness,
accuracy and reliability of 20% DF reporting in order to reinforce accountability.

We recommended to the Management, with the Municipal Accounting Office as lead the following:
1. Trace, as far as practicable, the period or date of acquisition/completion/recognition of the related PPEs and compute depreciation based on the estimated useful lives of these assets. Prepare adjusting entries, when warranted, to correct the over recognition of depreciation expenses. Prior years’ and current years’ depreciation adjustments should be directly recognized in the Statement of Changes in Net Equity as Prior Years Adjustment, and Statement of Financial Performance as Depreciation Expense, respectively.
2. Provide adequate depreciation for assets acquired during the year and recognized the related Depreciation Expense in the Statement of Financial Performance.
3. Faithfully observe the related guidelines on subsequent measurement of PPE to reinforce preparation of accurate and reliable financial reports, moving forward.
observations.managementAction: *Adjusting Entries were Fully Implemented made last April 2020 to correct the overstatement/recognition of prior year’s depreciation expense. Prior years’ and current years’ depreciation of PPE is now recognized on monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to ensure, as far as practicable, an optimum level of delivery of 20% DF and other initiatives through development and institution of enabling mechanisms that exacts the performance of responsibilities, enhances the periodic monitoring of implementation, and addresses the identified factors causing delays in project execution.
observations.managementAction: *Prior years’ PPAs are now prioritized by the administration for implementation which resulted to increase in the percentage of completion. LCE created a Project Monitoring Team to ensure that PPAs are being properly implemented and delivered. 20% DF and other infrastructure projects are now done thru Contracting or Bidding instead of By Administration to fast track the implementation. LCE created a functional Project Monitoring Team to ensure that PPAs are being properly implemented and delivered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management with the Sanggunian as leads, formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: *GFPS and TWG have convened to discuss the issues concerned on the implementation of PPAs for the formulation of GAD Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, with GFPS as leads, ensure, as far as practicable, an optimum level of delivery of GAD initiatives through the establishment of GAD monitoring and evaluation mechanisms that exacts the performance of responsibilities and enhances the periodic monitoring of implementation, in accordance with the requirements of Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
*GFPS will ensure that the TAP Process (Transparency, Accountability and Participatory) will be observed together with the institutionalization of GAD Monitoring and Evaluation.
*LGU will institute and enhance additional GAD mechanism to achieve the delivery of GAD initiatives.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing the level of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that affect the essential management processes.
observations.managementAction: *The LGU observed protocols and processes especially on the response for hydro-meteorological hazards. These protocols were adopted from the National Disaster response Protocol (NDRP) of the OCD. All operations of the response cluster based at the operation center where focal person of each member agency is assigned on a daily schedule. The MDRRMO is also reporting during the quarterly and emergency MDRRMC meetings. With the pandemic this year, all program implementation by the MDRRMO are coordinated with the Provincial DRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGUs of the province ensure the effective and efficient execution of disaster responses through the establishment of appropriately contextualized disaster protocols.
observations.managementAction: *The LGU observed protocols and processes especially on the response for hydro-meteorological hazards. These protocols were adopted from the National Disaster response Protocol (NDRP) of the OCD. All operations of the response cluster based at the operation center where focal person of each member agency is assigned on a daily schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that proper planning should be in place. The efforts of the municipality in planning its projects, programs and activities for DRRM should be coordinated with other developmental and environmental plans of the municipality in order to assure the integration of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: *Proper planning of programs under DRRMF was made to ensure proper implementation of plans and lessen number of realigned programs. Plans are also shown and manifested in a plan that is more developmental and comprehensive and integrated in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development council. Also, the team suggested that the plan should be coordinated with other developmental and environmental plans of the municipality in order to assure the integration of infrastructure projects such as, but not limited to, construction of embankments to eliminate flood risks. Regular monitoring of project implementation.

*Previous unimplemented projects from CY 2016 below are compressed to aim to be able to alleviate communities’ current living condition. 20% Development Fund and other infrastructure projects are now done thru “Contracting” or “Bidding” instead of “by administration” to fast track the implementation. Local Chief Executive created a Project Monitoring Team to closely check the MPDO and Engineering Office to ensure projects are properly implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each category of asset per fund, and to conduct periodic reconciliation of their respective records to forestall possible discrepancies. Any discrepancy should be immediately investigated and properly adjusted in the books to fairly present the said account in the financial statements; and
2. Require the Inventory Committee to ensure the completion of the comprehensive actual physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE to determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account at year-end.
observations.managementAction: *Efforts are undertaken by Management to fully comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:

GFPS and Management fully complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management, with the Municipal Accounting Office as lead,
agreed to the following:

a. Trace, as far as practicable, the period or date of
acquisition/completion/recognition of the related PPEs and compute
depreciation based on the estimated useful lives of these assets. Prepare
adjusting entries, when warranted, to correct the over recognition of
depreciation expense. Prior years’ and current years’ depreciation adjustments
should be directly recognized in the Statement of Changes in Net Equity as
Prior Years Adjustment, and Statement of Financial Performance as
Depreciation Expense, respectively;
b. Provide adequate depreciation for assets acquired during the year and
recognized the related Depreciation Expense in the Statement of Financial
Performance.
c. Faithfully observe the related guidelines on subsequent measurement of PPE to
reinforce preparation of accurate and reliable financial reports, moving forward.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure, as far as practicable, an
optimum level of delivery of initiatives through development and institution of enabling
mechanisms that exacts the performance of responsibilities, enhances the periodic
monitoring of implementation, and addresses the identified factors causing delays in
project execution.

We recommended and Management with the Sanggunian as leads, agreed to
formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS,
to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is
mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.

We recommended and Management, with GFPS as leads, agreed to ensure, as far
as practicable, an optimum level of delivery of GAD initiatives through the establishment
of GAD monitoring and evaluation mechanisms that exacts the performance of
responsibilities and enhances the periodic monitoring of implementation, in accordance
with the requirements of Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGUs of the province
collectively and individually ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM
responsibilities by enhancing the level of coordination through managing the challenges
on the dependencies that affect the essential management processes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGUs of the province ensure
the effective and efficient execution of disaster responses through the establishment of
appropriately contextualized disaster protocols.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that proper planning should be in place. The efforts of the municipality in planning its projects, programs and activities for DRRM should be shown and manifested in a plan that is more comprehensive and integrated in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development council. Also, the team suggested that the plan should be coordinated with other developmental and environmental plans of the municipality in order to assure the integration of infrastructure projects such as, but not limited to, construction of embankments to eliminate flood risks.
observations.managementAction: Proper planning of programs under DRRMF was made to ensure proper implementation of plans and programs and lessen the number of realigned programs. Plans are also coordinated with other developmental and environmental plans of the municipality in order to assure the integration of infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: The implementation of DRRMF Programs is on-going.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the LDRRMO submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. Also, the Municipal Treasurer effect transfer of the unexpended balance of DRRMF so that the Municipal Accountant can draw a journal entry voucher to present fairly the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each category of asset per fund, and to conduct periodic reconciliation of their respective records to forestall possible discrepancies. Any discrepancy should be immediately investigated and properly adjusted in the books to fairly present the said account in the financial statements; and
2. Require the Inventory Committee to ensure the completion of the comprehensive actual physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE to determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Efforts are undertaken by Management to fully comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Discuss with the Head of the Agency on actions to be taken to resolve this issue/problem.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the management furnish COA with copies of contracts and purchase orders and other pertinent documents such as but not limited to the bidding documents, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed, etc. relative thereto within five (5) days after its perfection for timely evaluation, review and inspection in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 and institute measures to efficiently and timely submit the required documents.

contracts and purchase orders are submitted.

observations.recommendation:
a. Have a centralized warehouse facility that could store safely all the materials and supplies of the municipality. Also,
b. Designate and/or appoint qualified personnel with permanent status of employment to be held responsible and accountable for the supplies and materials stored in a centralized warehouse.
c. Account and secure all materials owned by the municipality. Stored personal properties of municipal employees should be discarded from the government warehouse as such is violative to the provision of PD 1445.
d. Comply with the requirements set by the existing accounting policies and procedures on proper recording of receipt and issuance of inventory, forms were readily available from eNGAS and could be reproduced. Maintain, update and reconcile the records of GSO/Supply Officer and Accounting Office and eventually submit a report to the Audit Team.
e. Revisit the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System specifically on custodianship for guidance in storage, warehousing and inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: Discussed with the Head of Agency on actions to be taken; GSO to designate personnel; steps in place to secure materials and maintain records.
observations.reason: No centralized warehousing established yet; issues on personnel designation and full implementation of storage and recording procedures remain unresolved.

observations.recommendation:
a. The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; prepare/maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card; prepare a schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and disclose the total network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.
b. The General Services Office should also maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and render a Report on Local Road Network of the LGU at the end of the accounting period.
c. Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the accounting office and the general services office with complete description and cost segregation of road components.
observations.managementAction: Discussed with the head of agency on actions to resolve the issue; GSO to designate personnel and prepare required documentation.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers and complete documentation for the road network have not yet been fully prepared; actions to resolve are pending.

observations.recommendation:
The audit team recommended an overall assessment and evaluation of Municipal Treasury’s
collection system against the prescribed procedures in the Local Treasury Operations
Manual, existing rules and regulations and best practices in treasury operations. Also, the
responsible municipal officials shall endeavor to strengthen the internal control
procedures over cash and continuously monitor its operating effectiveness.
Specifically, the team recommended and the management agreed that:
1. Collectors who are assigned in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer or
within the proximity of the said office shall remit their collections to the
Liquidating Officer at the close of the business day; in the case of collectors
assigned to the field, where travel time from their places of assignment to the
Local Treasury Office is more than one (1) day, turnover of collections shall
be made at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00;
the Liquidating Officer remit the same to the Municipal Treasurer for
deposit to the AGDB; and properly accomplished RCDs shall be prepared in
all stages;
2. Following the LTOM, only one RPT Receipt shall be issued for the collection
of RPT, both basic and SEF counterpart so as to properly account for the
accountable forms and also to ensure the simultaneous deposit of the said
collection. The basic and SEF counterpart of the RPT collection shall be
reported in separate columns in one RCD. The municipal treasurer shall
deposit separately the basic and SEF counterpart to the corresponding bank
accounts of General Fund and Special Education Fund, respectively;
3. Only one stub of accountable forms shall be issued at a time to the collectors.
No subsequent issuance shall be made unless the previously issued
accountable forms have been liquidated to ensure that they are fully and
properly accounted for. Also, prepare and submit monthly the Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms to monitor the issuance and
liquidation of all accountable forms; and
4. Immediately reconcile issued and liquidated accountable forms for each
collector by comparing the list of all issued accountable forms and all RCDs
to determine if there are accountable forms which are not yet presented for
liquidation and demand them from the collectors.
We recommended the following measures to correct the observed deficiencies
which the management agreed to follow:
1. Bank Reconciliation Statements shall be prepared regularly to promptly
detect errors and other discrepancies between the balances in the books and
the bank; and
2. The accounting department shall make adjusting entries addressing these
reconciling items in order to present fairly the balance of Cash in Bank
account in the financial statements.

While we commend the municipality for coming up with a LDRRMP that
encompasses the four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management, we
recommended and the management agreed that proper planning should be in place.
The efforts of the municipality in planning its projects, programs and activities for
DRRM should be shown and manifested in a plan that is more comprehensive and
integrated in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and
policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development
council.14 Also, the team suggested that the plan should be coordinated with other
developmental and environmental plans of the municipality in order to assure the
integration of infrastructure projects such as, but not limited to, construction of
embankments that eliminate flood risks.






The team has also evaluated the municipality’s compliance with accounting and
reporting standards. The audit team’s records do not bear submissions of monthly Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, except the MDRRM Fund Utilization Report 2018
which was made available upon the team’s request.
The report shall be prepared and certified correct by the Local Accountant. The Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall submit the report on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.15 This report discloses the
amount of LDRRMF, NDRRMF transferred to LGU, funds received from other LGUs and
other sources and how they are utilized. Also, it helps in the monitoring of implementation
specifically the fiscal accomplishment and status of the fund. As one of the accountability
reports it is also consistent with transparency which is one of the aspects of good governance.
For the accounting of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the amount of
₱3,188,167.21, we noted that the said balance is not directly transferred to the trust fund
books. In its Notes to Financial Statements and in an interview, the municipal accountant
disclosed that the unutilized balance for the year 2018 is still in the General Fund. Transfer of
funds will be made in the first quarter of the succeeding year. We also noted that at year-end,
the municipal accountant made an entry, debiting “Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM
Funds to Trust Fund” and crediting “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF”, to effect the transfer
pending the deposit of the corresponding amount to trust fund bank account due to the
volume of transactions in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office during year-end. However, the
existing rule requires that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-
MOOE shall be transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-
DRRM” in the Trust Fund books.16
Thus, the entry on the books of General Fund should have been a debit to “Subsidy to
other funds” and a credit to “Cash.” On the other hand, the entry on Trust Fund books is a
debit to “Cash” and a credit to “Trust Liability-DRRMF.” With the entry made by the
municipal accountant, in effect, Trust Liabilities-DRRMF was recognized in General Fund’s
books and not on Trust Fund.
We recommended the following measures which the management agreed to
follow:
a. the LDRRMO submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF; and
b. the Municipal Treasurer effect the transfer of the unexpended balance of
DRRMF so that the Municipal Accountant can draw a journal entry voucher
to present fairly the accounts affected.

Therefore, in addition to the actions already taken by the municipality, we recommended the following:
a. Require the Municipal Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each category of asset per fund, and to conduct periodic reconciliation of their respective records to forestall possible discrepancies. Any discrepancy should be immediately investigated and properly adjusted in the books to fairly present the said account in the financial statements; and
b. Require the Inventory Committee to ensure the completion of the actual physical and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Conducted Physical inventory
observations.reason: The municipality is still yet to conduct a more comprehensive physical inventory and reconciliation of records to forestall possible discrepancies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
• Prepare bank reconciliation statements on a monthly basis for every depository account of the Municipality and effect the updated adjustments as the daily transactions of the municipality may be required to clear the reconciling items, in order to present fairly the account balances in the financial statements; and
• Make Bank representations with the depository/servicing banks for the early release of the bank statements to COA Office and other supporting documents such as but not limited to the bank debit/credit memos, paid checks, etc. or request for a bank snapshot which could be made available immediately.
Also, enroll in the “We Access program” of the bank, if any to have an updated access of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already enrolled in the “We Access Program” of the bank to have an updated access of the transactions.
observations.reason: Bank statements and supporting documents are being accessed/obtained and bank reconciliation statements are being prepared on a regular basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that each accountable officer and collection clerk should maintain Reports of Collections and Deposits properly by organizing and filing them completely to facilitate easy retrieval of records for validation and/or verification purposes.
Further, we recommended that each revenue collection clerk should maintain cashbook that will serve as permanent record of transactions for future references.
observations.managementAction: RCD are now filed in the Municipal Treasurer's Office; each collector/collection clerk maintain their own cashbooks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management improve its existing control in handling of collection and custody and issuance of official receipts in behalf of the regular collectors. Collection of revenues and custody and the regular issuance of official receipts should be handled by regularly appointed or designated collecting officer only. This will afford better protection of government funds.
Also recommended compliance with GAA provisions on minimum net take home pay for employees and that prior to approval of loans, the HR officer should prepare the pay slip to determine whether such loan should be approved.
observations.managementAction: Job order personnel are no longer allowed to handle collection and issuance of official receipts on behalf of the regular collectors.
observations.reason: This measure was implemented to prevent loss or wastage of government funds and to afford better protection of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be properly dedicated in maintaining an updated and correct Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and Report on obsolete, spoiled and cancelled accountable forms and submit the same to the Commission to trace the accuracy and completeness of the reported collection of the Collectors as well as to protect the Government for possible misappropriation of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms are now organized and filed; obsolete/spoiled/cancelled accountable forms are being reported and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to review and identify the nature of receivables accounts that are long overdue. The agency head may request for write-off of accounts that are long outstanding and against which no actual claims exist in accordance with Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Filed a formal request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts with COA with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that adjustments are taken up in the books of accounts reverting the invalid payables and rectifying the Government Equity account as required.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were reverted to Government Equity and rectifying entries were taken up in the books as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) direct the responsible offices and departments involved to faithfully observe the proper planning and budgeting procedures for the 20% Development Fund as provided in the local code and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 of the Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management; and
b) exert best effort to achieve high performance in prosecution of 20% DF PPAs by closely coordinating with the engineering office in all stages of project implementation, and in order to avoid unnecessary problems and to come up with achievable plans; and
c) cause to continually monitor and evaluate the implementation of the PPAs at least quarterly. Direct the Municipal Project Monitoring Team to ensure projects are properly implemented.
observations.managementAction: Previous projects from 2015 and 2016 were compressed/merged to be feasible to implement; coordination with MPDO and Engineering Office is closely monitored and implementation is ongoing.
observations.reason: On-going implementation of the 20% DF and other Infrastructure projects. Some projects remain unimplemented from prior years and require further coordination, approvals or sufficient funding.

observations.recommendation:
a. Prior to approval of loans, the HR officer should prepare the pay slip of the employee which will aid the approving authority in determining whether such loan should be approved or not.
b. The Municipal Accountant should certify the correct amount of the net take home pay of the employee before approval of loan.
c. Comply with the general provisions of GAA on the minimum allowed net take home pay of an employee.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality practices prior certification and coordination; Municipal Mayor issued memorandum to stop approval of loans without necessary certification. Compliance being observed moving forward.
observations.reason: Practice was reviewed and adjustments made; loans approval process updated to ensure compliance with GAA provisions.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Engineer and/or contractors to expedite the implementation in accordance with the target dates of completion and closely monitor the status of accomplishment of the projects funded by the National Government Agencies so that the beneficiaries of the projects could immediately enjoy the expected benefits. Create a monitoring team to supervise the implementation of all projects and determine those projects that were already completed and return to the source agency any unutilized balance or request for revision/amended POW for savings, if warranted, for proper liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Fast-tracked implementation of projects in accordance with target dates; closely monitored and beneficiaries being identified; some projects implemented while others pending due to personnel and equipment constraints.
observations.reason: Some projects were implemented and tracked; remaining projects delayed due to lack of personnel and heavy equipment in the Implementing Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management furnish COA with copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders and other pertinent documents such as but not limited to the bidding documents, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed; and other pertinent documents relative thereto for timely evaluation, review and inspection in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 and institute measures to efficiently and timely submit the required documents.
observations.managementAction: BAC Office is in the process of compliance; required documents and attachments are being compiled and submitted on a quarterly basis together with other pertinent documents; copies of contracts, purchase orders and supporting documents are now submitted to COA Office.
observations.reason: Delay was due to revamp of employees and adjustments in the procurement process; the BAC Office is in the process of compliance and submission of documents on a regular basis.

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, in addition to the actions already taken by the municipality, we
recommended the following:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer
to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for
each category of asset per fund, and to conduct periodic reconciliation
of their respective records to forestall possible discrepancies. Any
discrepancy should be immediately investigated and properly adjusted
in the books to fairly present the said account in the financial
statements; and
b. Require the Inventory Committee to ensure the completion of the
comprehensive actual physical and prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to determine the accuracy,
reliability and integrity of the PPE account at year-end.

We recommended the following:
 Prepare bank reconciliation statements on a monthly basis for every
depository account of the Municipality and effect the necessary
adjustments as may be required to clear the reconciling items, in order
to present fairly the account balances in the financial statements; and
 Make representations with the depository/servicing banks for the early
release of the bank statements and other supporting documents such as
bank debit/credit memos, paid checks, etc. or request for a bank
snapshot which could be made available immediately. Also, enroll in
the “We Access program” of the bank, if any to have an updated access
of the transaction.

We recommended that each accountable officer should maintain Reports of
Collections and Deposits properly by organizing and filing them completely
to facilitate easy retrieval of records for validation and/or verification
purposes.

Further, we recommended that each revenue collection clerk should
maintain cashbook that will serve as permanent record of transactions for
future references.

We recommended that the management improve its existing control in the
handling of cash collection and accountable forms. Collection of revenues
and the custody and issuance of official receipts should be handled by
regularly appointed or designated collecting officer only. This will afford
better protection of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be dedicated in
maintaining an updated and correct Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms and Report on obsolete, spoiled and cancelled
accountable forms and submit the same to the Commission to trace the
accuracy and completeness of the reported collection of the Collectors as
well as to protect the Government for possible misappropriation of
government funds.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to
review and identify the nature of Receivables accounts that are long overdue. The
agency head may request for write-off of account from the COA with all the
documents required under Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management ensure that adjustments are taken up in the
books of accounts reverting the invalid payables and rectifying the Government
Equity account as required.

Direct the MPDO to properly and economically plan out the proposed project by initially conducting actual visit on the proposed project site to determine the viability and/or accessibility and coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office for proper costing and estimate, including manpower and equipment needed in order to have an effective and economical projects.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing discussion with the Head of Agency, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Engineer and the Sanggunian on how to effectively implement all unfinished projects.

Requested the MPDC that all projects going forward will be funded accordingly and will not accommodate those small project proposals that will have no beneficial impact to the constituents.

Requested also the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile and finalize the balance of unutilized fund of all unimplemented projects.
observations.reason:
Cannot implement those projects that were given a small amount of budget/funding.

Lack of personnel and manpower.

Change administration (different priorities by the Head of Agency).

observations.recommendation:
Effectively plan the projects that would be implemented through the 20% DF. Conduct site visitation of targeted areas prior to proposal of project for proper evaluation and costing of project. Secure and verify necessary legal documents in connection with the proposed project.

Determine the viability, sustainability and accessibility of the projects to be implemented. Such should have an impact on the society and would be a way to alleviate the community’s current social status.

Have a centralized warehouse facility that could store safely all the materials and supplies of the municipality.

Designate and/or appoint qualified personnel with permanent status of employment to be held responsible and accountable for supplies and materials stored in a centralized warehouse.

Account and secure all materials owned by the municipality. Stored personal properties of municipal employees should be discarded from the government warehouse as such is violative to the provision of PD 1445.

Comply with the requirements set by the existing accounting policies and procedures on proper recording of receipt and issuance of inventory, forms were readily available from eNGAs and could be reproduced. Maintain, update and reconcile the records of GSO/Supply Officer and Accounting Office and eventually submit a report to the Audit Team.

Revisit the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System specifically on custodianship for guidance in storage, warehousing and inventory taking.

Strictly comply with and submit the documentary requirements stated under Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to establish the validity of claims of BAC honoraria.

Adhere to the guidelines set forth by DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A and 2007-3.

Prior to approval of loans, the HR officer should prepare the pay slip of the employee which will aid the approving authority in determining whether such loan should be approved or not.

The Municipal Accountant should certify the correct amount of the net take home pay of the individual employee before approval of loan.

Comply with the general provisions of the GAA on the minimum allowed net take home pay of an employee.

The management should abide with the rules and regulations regarding the timely payment of RATA and that in no time shall the allowances be given when services are not yet rendered. Accordingly, in case of leave of absence of officials and employees, the RATA should be pro-rated based on the actual days worked or it can be deducted from the succeeding month’s RATA. Also, RATA claims should be supported with complete documents as required under Section 5.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Prepare the RPCPPE and reconcile the actual physical count of property with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to the accounts. Also, facilitate the submission of the said report to the audit team for verification and validation.

Determine the composition of each PPE and prepare a computation for depreciation for a much reliable amount.

Evaluate the current status of PPE and determine whether they are still serviceable. Unserviceable items should be properly disposed observing the existing rules and guidelines on property disposal.

For inventory tagging, each item should have their individual tags even if the items were bought as a whole. Tags should be per PPE class for easier identification and should also indicate the AO’s name for monitoring.

The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; Prepare/maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D); Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and disclose the total network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements (Annex B).

The General Services Office should also maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (Annex E); prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System (Annex C); and render a Report on Local Road Network of the LGU at the end of the accounting period (Annex A).

Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the accounting office and the general services office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

The management should see to it that rules and regulations pertaining to granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances should be faithfully adhered to. The management should strictly enforce the liquidation/ collection of all cash advances within the allowable time frame to prevent misuse or loss of government funds and to properly/timely account the expenses in the period the expense was incurred.

Send demand letters to officials/employees who have unsettled cash advances after the prescribed period given by law; failure of the said officials/employees to settle his/her account would constitute a valid cause for the withholding of their salary and other sanction as stated in COA Circular No. 97-002.

For long-outstanding accounts, determine the collectability of each account. If found impossible to collect after exhausting efforts to recover the amount, the management may request for write-off of this account with duly supported necessary documents.

The Municipal Accountant should see to it that goods or services were already delivered or rendered and that all claims have complete supporting documents before recording it as part of agency's obligations.

Revert undocumented payables to the unappropriated surplus of the GF and any unliquidated balance of accounts payable which has been outstanding for two years or more.

Prepare the necessary adjusting and/or reclassification entry to properly reflect the correct amount of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Adjusting/reclassification entries have been made to properly reflect the correct amount of the accounts payable account.

On the unclaimed checks by the payee/claimant which became staled and have been outstanding for more than two years, adjustments have been made and reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.reason: N/A

observations.recommendation:
Review the CLUP and update the zoning ordinance of the municipality.

Conduct inventory of all land owned by the LGU.

Plan out efficiently the infrastructure projects, site locations should be properly identified to determine whether the location is a government land or whether there is a need to acquire the land or to secure deed of donation.

Secure land title/s or deed of donation before it will be considered in the AIP and must be in the position of the government before the implementation of infrastructure projects starts.

For contested/disputed areas, secure court order/s that the land was favorably awarded to the LGU before its inclusion in the AIP and the start of construction.
observations.managementAction:
Immediately stopped the implementation of the ALS Building upon knowing that there's still an on-going issue with the contested area.

Conducted the inventory of all real properties owned by the municipality and made sure that before the implementation of infrastructure project starts, all valid and legal documents are secured.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Engineer and/or contractors to expedite the implementation in accordance with the target dates of completion and closely monitor the status of accomplishment of the projects funded by the National Government Agencies so that the intended beneficiaries of the projects could immediately enjoy the expected benefits.  

Create a monitoring team to supervise the implementation of all projects and determine those projects that were already completed and return to the source agency any unutilized balance or request for revision/amended POW for savings, if warranted, for proper liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Fast-tracked the implementation of all projects from the National Government Agencies except for the Infrastructure projects due to the lack of personnel and heavy equipment in the Office of the Municipal Engineer and the bulk of 20% Local Development Projects.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that rules and regulations pertaining to granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances should be faithfully adhered to. The management should strictly enforce the liquidation /collection of all cash advances within the allowable time frame to prevent misuse or loss of government funds and to properly account the expenses in the period the expense was incurred. 

Cash advances for CIF should only be granted if the prior cash advance for the same purpose has already been liquidated and in accordance to COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

Send demand letters to officials/employees who have unsettled cash advances after the prescribed period given by law.
observations.managementAction:
Strictly enforced the liquidation of all cash advances within the allowable time frame or as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served. 

Additional cash advances were not allowed unless the previous cash advance given to him/her has been settled. Demand letters were also sent to those identified employees with unsettled balances.

Continuously remind the Special Disbursing Officer to liquidate the payroll on time and immediately refund any unclaimed salaries/wages.

Complied with the released COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 regarding the guidelines on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of Confidential and Intelligence Funds.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
Furnish the COA auditor with copies of delivery documents or inform/notify the auditor within 24 hours from acceptance of items for timely evaluation and inspection as provided for under Section 6.9 of COA Circular 2009-002. 

Prior to the distribution, the management should put its markings/brandings over the livestock for proper identification and to easily facilitate onward monitoring. This is also to established proprietary over the livestock distributed to the beneficiaries.  

Conduct periodic inspection to monitor the condition and existence of the distributed livestock for future reference and possible expansion of the same project.
observations.managementAction:
Duly inspected by the LGU Municipal Inspectorate Team upon arrival of livestock and before distribution to the beneficiaries.

Markings and brandings of livestock had already been conducted for proper identification.

Regular and periodic inspection of livestock, conducted quarterly.
observations.reason: N/A

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Engineer should provide COA not only listings of projects to be implemented on the current year but also those on-going projects funded from prior years' development fund, including those NGA-funded projects. 

The management should post notifications/billboards/signage at the least possible cost, to inform the public on the details of the project implementation. The billboard/signage should include necessary details about the implemented project, such as but not limited to, source of fund, date started, project duration and its target date of completion. 

Inform the COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
observations.managementAction:
Posted signage and billboard notifications on some infrastructure projects to inform the public on the details of the implementation.

Regular update to the COA on the status of project implementations.
observations.reason: N/A

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management furnish COA with copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders and other pertinent documents such as but not limited to the bidding documents, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed; etc. relative thereto within five (5) days after its perfection for timely evaluation, review and inspection in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 and institute measures to efficiently and timely submit the required documents.
observations.managementAction:
Delay was due to the revamp of employees in different offices and the newly assigned personnel for documentation is in process of adjustment.

The BAC Office is now in the process of compliance under this AOM.
observations.reason: Copy of contracts, purchase orders and other pertinent documents were included as attachments to the Disbursement Vouchers and thus submitted on a quarterly basis together with other reports submitted by Accounting Office.
